title: Asuka at the start of END OF EVANGELION
display_title: Asuka at the start of END OF EVANGELION
wikitext:
[]: Asuka at the start of END OF EVANGELION             Quote Post by Hard Angel » Wed Dec 20, 2006 3:20 pm how and why did she end up in a coma? I read online that after episode 24 that she had made a failed suicide attempt, which would seem to make sense... except that the episode itself doesn't confirm it. does that have something to do with the un-translated message pasted on the door of Asuka's hospital room?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 20, 2006 3:36 pm She wasn't in a comma she was sedated. Misato: Where's Asuka? Shigeru: In sickroom 303. (Shot of Asuka lying in bed) Misato (off screen): Put her into EVA-02 anyway. Maya: But her synchronization with Eva hasn't recovered yet. Misato: Yes, but she'll surely be killed if she stays there. The best place to hide her is inside EVA-02. Maya: Affirmative! (to headset) Stop medication to pilot . Prepare for launch!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 20, 2006 3:40 pm The downward spiral that is Asuka: Episode 19: Fails miserably to stop Zereul Episode 22: She gets mind raped by Arael Episode 23: Is completely unable to pilot her Eva, thereby negating any value she feels she might have had Episode 24: She goes out to some delapidated house to presumably waste away and die. Later taken into custody and presumably sedated.

[]:             Quote Post by Hard Angel » Wed Dec 20, 2006 3:45 pm I don't know of a psych drug... and I really, really dislike psych drugs... that zonk you out that bad. not to say that you have to square fiction with reality. it seems odd to me that they never supplied any exposition to explain Asuka's situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Dec 20, 2006 3:57 pm I dunno if the shot she gets when she attacks Shinji in the manga counts for anything.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 20, 2006 4:08 pm I got ODed on Ativan (spelling?) once and it knocked me out for a full day, just another reason why the Charter hospital chain sucked. Wiki may actually be useful here. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sedation

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 20, 2006 4:39 pm Hard Angel wrote: it seems odd to me that they never supplied any exposition to explain Asuka's situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Hard Angel » Wed Dec 20, 2006 5:12 pm oh, I definitely understood Asuka's character arc and backstory, I meant more the specific circumstances while in the hospital. that lacked expositon to explain it (IMHO). sometimes plot developments catch me off guard and I got confused with the various groups and organizations at times (principally the difference between NERV and Gerhirn -- had a lot of trouble understanding that) but I do understand how characters change. I tend not to have more trouble mentally parsing events and details ratehr than character-based stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Auriel » Wed Dec 20, 2006 5:53 pm Well, IMHO she was not in a coma, but in a catatonic state which often happens after very traumatic events. Though I'm sure she was also drugged.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 20, 2006 5:58 pm Doesn't quite fit catÂ·aÂ·toÂ·niÂ·a (kāt'ə-tō'nē-ə) Pronunciation Key n. An abnormal condition variously characterized by stupor, stereotypy, mania, and either rigidity or extreme flexibility of the limbs. It is most often associated with schizophrenia.

[]:             Quote Post by Auriel » Wed Dec 20, 2006 7:16 pm Well, if you ask me, everyone is Eva is pretty schizophrenic

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 20, 2006 7:38 pm schizÂ·oÂ·phreÂ·niÂ·a (skĭt'sə-frē'nē-ə, -frěn'ē-ə) Pronunciation Key n. Psychiatry. Also called dementia praecox. a severe mental disorder characterized by some, but not necessarily all, of the following features: emotional blunting, intellectual deterioration, social isolation, disorganized speech and behavior, delusions, and hallucinations. schizÂ·oÂ·phreÂ·niÂ·a (skĭt'sə-frē'nē-ə, -frěn'ē-ə) Pronunciation Key n. Any of a group of psychotic disorders usually characterized by withdrawal from reality, illogical patterns of thinking, delusions, and hallucinations, and accompanied in varying degrees by other emotional, behavioral, or intellectual disturbances. Schizophrenia is associated with dopamine imbalances in the brain and may have an underlying genetic cause.

[]:             Quote Post by Auriel » Wed Dec 20, 2006 7:44 pm How so? Any of a group of psychotic disorders usually characterized by withdrawal from reality, illogical patterns of thinking, delusions, and hallucinations, and accompanied in varying degrees by other emotional, behavioral, or intellectual disturbances

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 20, 2006 7:55 pm Asuka is a narcissist, Shinji isn't delusional or prone to hallucinations unless there are unusual circimstances at play and not all of those are delusions or hallucinations

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:12 pm The other day I looked up NPD, and zomg, Asuka is most definataly in the starting stages of that if she doesn't fully suffer from it already. :shock: Shinji, however, seems to suffer from both(?) depresion and anxiety (they tend to go hand and hand, so yeah, I guess he suffers both.) Asuka's downward spiral (including what people already elborated on), as well as her eventual recovery: Episode 15: Shinji not acting emotionally receptive towards her when they kiss. Episode 16: Shinji beating Asuka's Synch Ratio. The Eva Monkey wrote: The downward spiral that is Asuka: Episode 19: Fails miserably to stop Zereul Episode 22: She gets mind raped by Arael Episode 23: Is completely unable to pilot her Eva, thereby negating any value she feels she might have had

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:31 pm Auriel wrote: Well, if you ask me, everyone is Eva is pretty schizophrenic

[]:             Quote Post by captainhaddock » Wed Dec 20, 2006 10:48 pm I do not think Shinji is schizophrenic, that's usually characterized by some form of delusions. More likely he would be unipolar.

[]:             Quote Post by Hard Angel » Thu Dec 21, 2006 1:03 am Auriel wrote: Well, IMHO she was not in a coma, but in a catatonic state which often happens after very traumatic events. Though I'm sure she was also drugged.

[]:             Quote Post by Hard Angel » Thu Dec 21, 2006 1:15 am Auriel, psychiatry doesn't say "pretty schizophrenic". according to psychiatry (which I don't consider scientific, but according to that belief system) you "have" "schizophrenia" or not. I hope (this goes for everyone) that before you start discussing schizophrenia around that you have a thorough knowledge of it, not just one culled from Webster's or Wikipedia or dictionary.com. only a little research (and I don't mean just reading a Wikipedia article, but eight or ten hours of study) to rectify that lack of knowledge. about psychiatry, I believe that much of it makes no sense. you can not learn not to have a medical disorder. however, you can learn not to "have" a psychiatric disorder (I won't use scare quotes: psychiatric "disorder"). it can long work. you can do it, though. tell me about a terminal illness you can learn not to have. I think that blows one of psychiatry's principles away, right there. clearly the medical model does not apply and yet psychiatry continues to employ it. as a person in her late 30's it really distresses me to see younger people (and I guess that makes up most of the people on this messageboard) eating up these half-truths. of course, people my age and older do, too. however, the younger the age of the person, the more a belief gets ingrained. I hope you all will examine psychiatry critically and deconstruct it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Dec 21, 2006 2:33 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Episode 26/26': Asuka returns to reality because she has the will to live on.
